'''Tumnors''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of large flying predators that lived on the [[Da Soocha V|fifth]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Da Soocha]], which was located in the [[Cyax system/Legends|Cyax system]] of [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> With a five [[Meter/Legends|meter]] wing span the creatures could fly and were able to attack prey with both their [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] and claws. Tumnor hunted the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Ixll]] species also native to their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and were especially fond of devouring their young. Due to their role as the species natural hunters the Tumnor featured prominently in Ixll legend and always took on an evil and merciless role. Whilst the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] maintained a presence on the moon during the [[Operation Shadow Hand]] part of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], Republic scientists stationed their never actually sighted a Tumnor. Dar Soocha V was destroyed in 10 ABY by the [[Galaxy Gun]], a world–devastating [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] super weapon.

==Behind the scenes==
The Tumnor were first mentioned in [[1993]] in the ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' a supplement written by [[Michael Allen Horne]] for [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]], a roleplaying game produced by [[West End Games]]. They also received mention in the reference books, ''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' written by [[Daniel Wallace]] in [[1998]] and the [[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded|third edition]] of ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'' written in [[2000]] by [[Bill Slavicsek]]. No source has made clear if the Tumnor were sentient or if any survived the destruction of their homeworld.
